channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: After rolling out Sora beyond the US, OpenAI plans to put the video AI tool inside ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT can already generate text and images, and it seems video will be added to the list eventually.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: De'Longhi's new bean-to-cup coffee machine could make you a milk-frothing maestro
item.description: Love a latte but struggle to make them at home? The De'Longhi La Specialista Touch might be the solution.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Who at DOGE has access to U.S. intelligence secrets? Democrats are demanding answers
item.description: Democratic lawmakers demanded answers from billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency on Thursday as concerns about who has access to America’s most sensitive information continue to dog Trump administration efforts to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Next-gen nuclear startup plans 30 reactors to fuel Texas data centers
item.description: Last Energy, a nuclear upstart backed by an Elon Musk-linked venture capital fund, says it plans to construct 30 microreactors on a site in Texas to supply electricity to data centers across the state.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Around $40 billion worth of illicit crypto transactions took place in 2024
item.description: Record figure is likely to rise even higher in 2025 as new information on historical activity emerges.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is this the perfect portable router? GL-iNet's latest model offers dual SIM, load balancing, gigabit LAN ports and four massive antennas
item.description: GL.iNet’s Spitz Plus GL-X2000 merges dual SIM speeds with bandwidth bonding, offering a fast, stable Wi-Fi 6 connection for mobile users and remote locations.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google’s AI summaries are changing search. Now it’s facing a lawsuit
item.description: For more than two decades, users have turned to search engines like Google, typed in a query, and received a familiar list of 10 blue links—the gateway to the wider web. Ranking high on that list, through search engine optimization (SEO), has become a $200 billion business.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011 and which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Finally, we have some GPU competition - AMD announces the Radeon RX 9070 XT March 6 launch date, starting at $599 alongside the RX 9070 at $549
item.description: After months of waiting, AMD has finally unveiled the Radeon RX 9070 series GPUs and their pricing, where its XT variant looks set to be the winner of this generation's budget GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Prioridades de los líderes TI en materia de IA: productividad por encima de la innovación
item.description: Los líderes de TI siguen apuntando a lo más fácil cuando utilizan la inteligencia artificial (IA), centrándose en mejorar la productividad de los empleados en lugar de dar rienda suelta a usos más avanzados como permitir la innovación o aumentar los ingresos, según una nueva encuesta.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Amazon claims to cut quantum computing timeline by 5 years with new Ocelot chip
item.description: Amazon Web Services has unveiled a new quantum computing chip, Ocelot, that the company claims could reduce error correction costs by up to 90% compared to traditional approaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is retrieval-augmented generation? More accurate and reliable LLMs
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a technique used to “ground” large language models (LLMs) with specific data sources, often sources that weren’t included in the models’ original training.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists scorn EPA push to say climate change isn't a danger, say just look around at the world
item.description: As President Donald Trump's administration looks to reverse a cornerstone finding that climate change endangers human health and welfare, scientists say they need to look around because it’s obvious how bad global warming is and how its getting worse.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta apologizes for 'error' that resulted in violent content flooding Instagram feeds
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, apologized Thursday for a technical error that resulted in some users' Reels feeds being filled with graphic or violent content.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Five takeaways from Cisco Live EMEA
item.description: Cisco held its European user event in Amsterdam this month, presenting a good mix of industry trends, product innovation, and vision from Cisco as to where they see the market going.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cot framework aims to ease Rust web development
item.description: Web developers looking for an easier time with the Rust language may want to check out Cot, an emerging web framework pitched as being for “lazy developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This is what no one planned could happen after taking a DNA test
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says DNA tests pose privacy risks like shared genetic information, inaccuracies and hard data deletion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
